rows.evidence: contracts.py:107 literally comments '# heading_path' next to section_path; live probe (PLAN_CRITIQUE_2026-07-13.md section 2) shows heading_path non-empty on 86-100% of parents per corpus.

rows.collision_note: Failure mode (a): 'parent' means three things — (1) parent_chunk; (2) TreeNode.parent_ids (summary_tree.py:95) which are MEMBER parent-chunk ids, NOT tree-parents (tree children live in child_node_ids); (3) qdrant passthrough_parent_ids (parent-chunk ids again). A reader assuming parent_ids = tree ancestry will invert the hierarchy.
rows.evidence: summary_tree.py:95-96: parent_ids '# L1 members (rollups)' vs child_node_ids '# tree children' — same row, both 'parent/child' words, orthogonal meanings.

rows.collision_note: Failure mode (a): 'child' spans three id spaces — child_chunk ids (chunk_id), summary provenance source_child_ids (P0.1 'verify summary has child IDs' means THESE), and tree child_node_ids (P0.2 'one-child section' means THESE, they are rollup node ids). The Mongo collection for child chunks is named plain 'chunks', hiding the parent/child asymmetry (parent_chunks vs chunks).
rows.evidence: P0.1 checklist line 367 vs P0.2 line 385 use 'child' for different stores; contracts.py:128 chunk_type enum child|summary|doc_summary.

rows.collision_note: Failure mode (a), strong: 'Singleton' appears ~20x in backend meaning service-instance singletons (auth.py:294, llm.py:1237, tool_registry.py:215, facet_tagger.py:42...). The P0.2 open decision 'future singleton sections physically omitted or retained as aliases' (checklist:388-390) is PLAN-ONLY — no omit/alias branch exists in summary_tree.py; today every one-child section is retained and stamped with passthrough fields.
rows.evidence: grep -rn singleton backend/services: zero tree-related hits; the concept lives entirely under the passthrough_* names.

Plan S3 codifies it: 'page_start is absent everywhere and only a reingest lane can ever add it'. Any new plan item that 'adds page anchors' would duplicate this existing plumbing instead of feeding it.
rows.evidence: Only the ocr_ast branch (tier_chunker.py:1429-1473) passes non-None values; heading-bound tiers A/B/B+ never set it.

Wiring risk: implementing seat-descent as a new path instead of feeding reserved doc_ids into the existing SummaryTreeNavigator would be failure mode (b).
rows.evidence: summary_tree_navigator.py:503 names the strategy; retriever/__init__.py:2225 comment 'derived probes may narrow through Tier-0 and the RAPTOR tree'.

rows.collision_note: Failure mode (b), explicit: the built tree is NOT RAPTOR — RAPTOR_RAG_CRITIQUE_AND_ROADMAP.md:39 calls it 'closer to a doc-summary index + auto-merging than to RAPTOR' (no clustering, doc-gated). Meanwhile P3.1 plans a real clustering-based 'thematic RAPTOR layer' (checklist:1094,1124) as future work. Two different concepts share the name RAPTOR: the existing heading-grouped summary_tree (misnomer) and the to-be-built thematic layer. A grep-driven implementer could 'extend RAPTOR' in either place.
rows.evidence: checklist:1124 'Only after success may the architecture claim a thematic RAPTOR layer' — the ledger itself acknowledges the current tree does not earn the name.

rows.collision_note: Failure mode (a): 'summary' spans two levels — parent summaries (chunk_type='summary', Funnel A breadth, Ghost A parent_summary.v1) vs document summaries (chunk_type='doc_summary', Tier-0 routing only, '§10.2: routing card, NEVER evidence'). The owner-architecture line 'doc_summaries = only summary vectors' reads ambiguously against the live schema where parent-summary vectors dominate the naive collection. Plan phrase 'document summary tree' (checklist:1239) means the whole per-doc summary_tree, a third referent.
rows.evidence: tier0.py:2-10 docstring states the card/evidence split; contracts.py:128 keeps the two as distinct enum values.

P0.1's open verify item and P0.2's open fixture item both say 'boundaries' but target different stores (summary provenance vs tree node extents); implementing one validator for both would be wrong, implementing two under the same name invites failure mode (b).
rows.evidence: grep -rn boundar backend/services returns ghost_a prompt, chunker splitters, schema_validators architectural sense, and answerability_tuning.py:116 coverage-boundary — all unrelated.

rows.collision_note: If this term denotes headers appearing mid-flow that should split/route chunks, that behavior already exists across THREE seams (synthetic injection pre-parse, docling section_heading blocks, page-heading demotion in the tree). A new 'mid-document header' feature built fresh would be failure mode (b); it should be specified as a change to one of these seams. The open plan work nearest to it is P0.5 'Audit OCR-corrupt heading paths and define a source-safe repair rule' (checklist:462) and plan S3's heading/OCR quality audit.
rows.evidence: grep -rniE 'mid.document' over docs/, CONTINUITY/, backend/, frontend/ returns zero hits.
